char fread_letter(FILE *fp).brief: Reads and returns the next non-whitespace character from the given file stream.
char fread_letter(FILE *fp).details: This function reads characters sequentially from the provided FILE pointer until a non-whitespace character is encountered. It then returns this character. The function does not modify the file position beyond consuming whitespace characters, and it assumes the FILE pointer is valid and open for reading. It is useful for parsing input where leading whitespace should be ignored.

params.fp: Pointer to the FILE stream from which characters are read.

char fread_letter(FILE *fp).returns: The first non-whitespace character read from the file stream.
char fread_letter(FILE *fp).name: fread_letter

void fread_to_eol(FILE *fp).brief: Reads and discards characters from the given file stream until the end of the current line, then positions the stream at the start of the next line.
void fread_to_eol(FILE *fp).details: This function consumes characters from the provided FILE pointer until it encounters a newline ('\n') or carriage return ('\r') character, effectively skipping the current line. After reaching the end of the line, it continues to read and discard any subsequent newline or carriage return characters to position the stream at the beginning of the next line. The stream is left in a state ready for reading from the start of the following line. It does not return any value and assumes the FILE pointer is valid and open for reading.

params.fp: Pointer to a FILE object from which characters are read and to which the stream position is advanced.

void fread_to_eol(FILE *fp).returns: Void; the function does not return a value but modifies the file stream position to the start of the next line.
void fread_to_eol(FILE *fp).name: fread_to_eol

int fread_number(FILE *fp).brief: Reads an integer value from a file stream, handling optional signs and special formatting.
int fread_number(FILE *fp).details: This function reads characters from the provided FILE pointer until a non-whitespace character is encountered. It then processes optional '+' or '-' signs to determine the sign of the number. If the next character is not a digit, it logs a bug message and returns zero. Otherwise, it reads consecutive digits to construct the integer value. If a '|' character is encountered, it recursively reads and adds another number from the stream. If the character is not a space, it pushes the character back onto the stream. The function returns the parsed integer, considering any sign and special formatting.

params.fp: A pointer to the FILE object representing the input stream from which the number is read.

int fread_number(FILE *fp).returns: The integer value read from the file stream, with sign and special formatting applied.
int fread_number(FILE *fp).name: fread_number

const Flags fread_flag(FILE *fp).brief: Reads a flag set from a file stream and returns it as a Flags object.
const Flags fread_flag(FILE *fp).details: This function reads a single word from the specified file stream using fread_word, then constructs and returns a Flags object initialized with that word. It serves as a utility to load flag configurations from files, enabling the program to interpret and manipulate flag states based on stored data. The function assumes the input stream contains a valid representation of flags as a single word.

params.fp: Pointer to the FILE object representing the input file stream from which the flag data is read.

const Flags fread_flag(FILE *fp).returns: A Flags object initialized with the word read from the file stream, representing a set of flags.
const Flags fread_flag(FILE *fp).name: fread_flag

String fread_string_eol(FILE *fp).brief: Reads a line from the given file until a newline character is encountered.
String fread_string_eol(FILE *fp).details: This function reads characters from the specified FILE pointer until a newline character ('\n') is found, effectively reading a single line from the file. It utilizes the fread_string function with the newline character as the delimiter, returning the read string excluding the newline. It is useful for line-by-line file processing where each line is treated as a separate string.

params.fp: Pointer to the FILE object from which the line is read.

String fread_string_eol(FILE *fp).returns: A String object containing the characters read from the file up to (but not including) the newline character.
String fread_string_eol(FILE *fp).name: fread_string_eol

String fread_word(FILE *fp).brief: Reads and returns the next word from a file stream, handling quoted strings and whitespace.
String fread_word(FILE *fp).details: This function reads characters from the provided FILE pointer until it encounters a non-whitespace character. If the character is a quote (' or "), it reads a quoted string; otherwise, it reads a word until whitespace or the specified end character. The read word is stored in a String object and returned. If the word exceeds the maximum allowed length (MIL), it logs a bug message. The function modifies the file stream position to after the read word.

params.fp: Pointer to the FILE stream from which to read the next word.

String fread_word(FILE *fp).returns: A String object containing the next word read from the file stream, or an empty String if an error occurs.
String fread_word(FILE *fp).name: fread_word

void fappend(const String &file, const S.brief: Appends the specified string to the given file.
void fappend(const String &file, const S.details: The function fappend opens the specified file in append mode and writes the provided string to it. If the string is empty, the function performs no action. If the file cannot be opened, it logs an error message using the Logging::bugf function. This function does not return a value and operates silently unless an error occurs during file opening.

params.file: The name or path of the file to which the string will be appended.
params.str: The string content to append to the file.

void fappend(const String &file, const S.returns: Void; the function performs its operation without returning a value.
void fappend(const String &file, const S.name: fappend

const char * dizzy_ctime(time_t *timep).brief: Returns a formatted string representing the local time corresponding to the given time_t value.
const char * dizzy_ctime(time_t *timep).details: This function converts the provided time_t pointer to a local time structure and formats it into a human-readable string, which is stored in a static buffer. The formatted string includes the day of the week, month, day of the month, time (hours, minutes, seconds), and year, ending with a newline character. The returned pointer points to a static buffer, so the content is overwritten on subsequent calls.

params.timep: Pointer to a time_t object representing the time to be converted and formatted.

const char * dizzy_ctime(time_t *timep).returns: A pointer to a static character array containing the formatted local time string.
const char * dizzy_ctime(time_t *timep).name: dizzy_ctime

time_t dizzy_scantime(const String &ctim.brief: Converts a formatted date/time string into a time_t value representing the corresponding local time.
time_t dizzy_scantime(const String &ctim.details: This function parses a date/time string in a specific format, extracts components such as day, month, year, hour, minute, and second, and converts them into a time_t value representing local time. It initializes a tm structure with the current local timezone settings, updates its fields based on the parsed string, and handles errors by logging bug reports if parsing fails or if the month is invalid. The function returns the resulting time_t value, which can be used for time comparisons or calculations.

params.ctime: A String object containing the date/time string to be parsed, expected in a specific format (e.g., 'Wed Feb  3 14:05:06 2021').

time_t dizzy_scantime(const String &ctim.returns: A time_t value representing the parsed local time; if parsing fails, it returns the time_t corresponding to the initialized tm structure.
time_t dizzy_scantime(const String &ctim.name: dizzy_scantime
